description_long: This object is a heavy, turret-mounted rotary rifle resembling a Gatling gun, featuring six parallel barrels arranged in a circular configuration. The barrels are metallic and copper-toned, extending from a robust, angular housing with a dark, industrial finish and gold accents. The main body is boxy, with visible mechanical gears on one side, suggesting a steampunk or industrial aesthetic. The base is wide and stable, with a combination of black and gold metallic panels, designed to anchor the weapon securely to the ground. The top of the housing has a prominent gold cap, and the overall construction appears reinforced for durability and sustained fire. The design is imposing and utilitarian, suitable for defensive or offensive deployment in urban environments.
description: A turret-mounted, rotary-barreled rifle with a steampunk aesthetic, featuring gold and black metallic finishes and visible mechanical gears.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the six copper barrels and the angular, gold-accented housing atop a sturdy, flared base.
description_view_1: The rear view reveals the mechanical gears on the side of the housing and the broad, gold-trimmed base.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the full length of the barrels and the robust, industrial body.
description_view_3: The front view focuses on the circular arrangement of the barrels, emphasizing the weapon's rotary design.
